# Module 3 homework: Internal measures of performance

This Chapter will focus on the topics surrounding Internal measures of performance

Topics includes:

- Apparent error
- Estimating the error rate and its uncertainty
- K-fold CV
-- Bootstrap
-- 0.632 & 0.632+
- Optimism

  

# Module 3 homework
Module 3 homework
1. In your repo, create HW3.qmd and add it to your book (_quarto.yml).
2. Calculate the apparent MAE and external MAE for all 100 models in m using their corresponding training datasets in td. Create a scatter plot with apparent MAE on the x-axis and external MAE on the y-axis. Use a large external dataset to calculate MAE. Describe what you observe.

# Installing the properties and packages if needed:
# ```{r}
install.packages("pak", quiet = TRUE)
pak::pak("thomasgstewart/ds6021d")
library(ds6021d)

# 2.) Calculate the apparent MAE and external MAE for all 100 models in m
# using their corresponding training datasets in td.
# Create a scatter plot with apparent MAE on the x-axis and external MAE on
# the y-axis. Use a large external dataset to calculate MAE.
# Describe what you observe.
vector_for_apparent_MAE_100_models <- rep(NA, 100)
vectors_for_external_MAE_100_models <- rep(NA, 100)
set.seed(2343)


for(index in seq_along(vector_for_apparent_MAE_100_models)){
    m_functions <- m[[index]]
    td_internal_training_data <- td[[index]]
    
    td_internal_training_predition_data_funct_y_out <- m_functions(td_internal_training_data)
    td_internal_training_y_data <- td_internal_training_data$y
    apparent_rate_MAE <- mean(abs(td_internal_training_predition_data_funct_y_out - td_internal_training_y_data))
    vector_for_apparent_MAE_100_models[index] <- apparent_rate_MAE
    
    external_data_vals <- external_data(10000)  # larger external set
    external_data_vals_funct_y_out <- m_functions(external_data_vals)
    external_rate_MAE <- mean(abs(external_data_vals_funct_y_out - external_data_vals$y))
    vectors_for_external_MAE_100_models[index] <- external_rate_MAE
}

plot(x = vector_for_apparent_MAE_100_models,
     y = vectors_for_external_MAE_100_models,
     main = "Apparent MAE vs External MAE",
     xlab = "Apparent MAE (Training)",
     ylab = "External MAE (Test)",
     pch = 19,
     col = "steelblue",
     cex = 1.2)

# 3.) For each of the 100 models, compute the optimism
# for MAE (apparent MAE minus external MAE).
# Plot the distribution of optimism.
# Calculate the mean optimism for MAE.
# Is the distribution roughly symmetric or skewed?
# What does the figure tell you about using apparent error
# as a performance estimate?
vector_for_optimism_MAE_100_models <- rep(NA, 100)
for(index in seq_along(vector_for_apparent_MAE_100_models)){
    vector_for_optimism_MAE_100_models[index] <- vector_for_apparent_MAE_100_models[index] - vectors_for_external_MAE_100_models[index]
}
mean_optimism_for_MAE_100_models <- mean(vector_for_optimism_MAE_100_models)

hist(vector_for_optimism_MAE_100_models,
     main = "Distribution of Optimism (Apparent MAE - External MAE)",
     xlab = "Optimism for MAE",
     col = "lightblue",
     border = "darkblue")
abline(v = mean_optimism_for_MAE_100_models, col = "red", lwd = 2)
text(x = mean_optimism_for_MAE_100_models,
     y = -0.5,
     labels = paste("Mean =", round(mean_optimism_for_MAE_100_models, 5)),
     col = "red")

# 4.) Select td[[1]] as your working dataset.
# Implement 10-fold CV for the regression model
# in the section 3 examples and record the resulting
# MAE estimate.
new_train_data <- td[[1]]
number_of_rows_training_data <- nrow(new_train_data)
k_val <- 10
set.seed(123)
folds_randomized <- sample(rep(1:k_val, length.out = number_of_rows_training_data))

cv_mae <- rep(NA, k_val)
for(k in 1:k_val){
    val_idx_of_CV <- which(folds_randomized == k)
    train_idx_of_CV <- which(folds_randomized != k)
    
    train_fold_of_CV <- new_train_data[train_idx_of_CV, ]
    val_fold_of_CV <- new_train_data[val_idx_of_CV, ]
    
    linear_model_of_fold_of_CV <- lm(y ~ ., data = train_fold_of_CV)
    predict_y_val_of_fold_of_CV <- predict(linear_model_of_fold_of_CV, newdata = val_fold_of_CV)
    
    cv_mae[k] <- mean(abs(predict_y_val_of_fold_of_CV - val_fold_of_CV$y))
    
    cat("10-fold CV for the regression model: Fold number: ", k, ", MAE =", round(cv_mae[k], 4), "\n")
}

# mean of 10-fold CV for MAE
mean_of_10_fold_CV_for_MAE <- mean(cv_mae)
print(mean_of_10_fold_CV_for_MAE)

# 5.) Using td[[1]], implement the bootstrap optimism estimator with B=200
# bootstrap samples. Report the apparent MAE, the estimated optimism,
# and the corrected MAE.
# Compare the corrected MAE to the true external error (computed with a large external dataset).
b_val <- 200
set.seed(42352)
optimism_b <- rep(NA, b_val)

m_function <- m[[1]]
new_training_predition_data_funct_y_output <- m_function(new_train_data)

apparent_error_mae <- mean(abs(new_training_predition_data_funct_y_output - new_train_data$y))  # using original m[[1]]

for (b in 1:b_val){
    idx_training_data <- sample(1:number_of_rows_training_data, replace = TRUE)
    bootstrap_training_data <- new_train_data[idx_training_data, ]
    
    linearly_fittted_model_b_value <- lm(y ~ ., data = bootstrap_training_data)
    
    # In-sample on bootstrap
    predicted_y_value_b_value_training_data_bootstrap_in_sample <- predict(linearly_fittted_model_b_value, newdata = bootstrap_training_data)
    mae_optimism_in_sample_b_value <- mean(abs(predicted_y_value_b_value_training_data_bootstrap_in_sample - bootstrap_training_data$y))
    
    # original training data
    predicted_y_value_b_value_training_data_bootstrap_original <- predict(linearly_fittted_model_b_value, newdata = new_train_data)
    mae_optimism_original_b_value <- mean(abs(predicted_y_value_b_value_training_data_bootstrap_original - new_train_data$y))
    
    # optimism for bootstrap formula
    optimism_b[b] <- mae_optimism_in_sample_b_value - mae_optimism_original_b_value
}

# corrected optimism bootstrap MAE
bootstrap_corrected_mae <- apparent_error_mae - mean(optimism_b)

# True external error
large_external <- external_data(20000)
true_external_error_mae <- mean(abs(predict(lm(y ~ ., data = new_train_data), newdata = large_external) - large_external$y))

cat("Apparent MAE:", round(apparent_error_mae, 5), "\n")
cat("Bootstrap optimism:", round(mean(optimism_b), 5), "\n")
cat("Corrected MAE:", round(bootstrap_corrected_mae, 5), "\n")
cat("True external MAE:", round(true_external_error_mae, 5), "\n")

hist(optimism_b,
     main = "Bootstrap estimates of optimism",
     xlab = "Optimism (in-sample MAE − original MAE)",
     freq = FALSE)
abline(v = mean(optimism_b), col = "blue", lwd = 2)
text(x = mean(optimism_b),
     y = 0,
     labels = paste("Mean =", round(mean_optimism, 5)),
     col = "blue")
# ```
